steps.title: Find the scale factor from the perimeter ratio
steps.content:
For similar triangles, corresponding sides are in the same ratio as their perimeters. From the table, triangle $TUV$ has perimeter 30 and triangle $XYZ$ has perimeter 90.
$$\text{Scale factor} = \frac{\text{Perimeter of } XYZ}{\text{Perimeter of } TUV} = \frac{90}{30} = 3$$
This means each side of triangle $XYZ$ is 3 times the corresponding side of triangle $TUV$.

steps.title: Apply the scale factor to find XY
steps.content:
Since $\overline{TU}$ corresponds to $\overline{XY}$, and the scale factor is 3:
$$XY = TU \times 3 = 9 \times 3 = 27$$
That matches choice C — C is correct.
